themes.heading: Medical emergencies
themes.text: Call 177 or go to the hospital. Depending on your mobile network, call: 1300, 0174 or 0880 for SOS Médecins. Operators may only speak French.

themes.heading: Safety
themes.text: There have been reports of violent crime, especially in Libreville and Port Gentil. Incidents include robberies and armed attacks. Don't walk alone or at night. Avoid isolated areas, including beaches. Protests and civil unrest may occur with little warning and may turn violent. Avoid large public gatherings and government buildings. Avoid unnecessarily attracting the attention of local authorities. Petty crimes, including pickpocketing, bag snatching and vehicle break-ins are common in Gabon. Keep your valuables out of sight. Flooding occurs during the rainy seasons. Check local conditions before driving. You may need a 4WD vehicle on some roads. Full travel advice: Safety

themes.heading: Health
themes.text: Insect-borne diseases such as yellow fever, zika and malaria occur throughout the year. Ensure your accommodation is insect-proof, use insect repellent and take anti-malarial medication. HIV/AIDS is common. Take precautions if you are taking part in high-risk activities. Foodborne, waterborne and other infectious diseases include cholera, typhoid and hepatitis. Drink only boiled or bottled water. Avoid raw or undercooked food. Medical facilities are adequate in major cities but basic or unavailable in rural areas. If you are seriously ill or injured, you will need medical evacuation. Ensure your travel insurance covers this. Full travel advice: Health

themes.heading: Local laws
themes.text: Ensure you always carry identification and evidence of the purpose of your visit. Local security forces may interpret laws broadly and inconsistently. You could break the law without intending to. Don't use or carry illegal drugs. Penalties include long prison sentences. Be careful when taking photos. It is illegal to photograph or film airports, the Presidential Palace, military sites, government buildings or border posts. It is illegal to buy or sell endangered wildlife products, including rhino horn and ivory, without a licence. Same-sex relationships aren't legally recognised. There have been attempts to charge people in same-sex marriages under public order and obscenity laws. Avoid public displays of affection. Full travel advice: Local laws

themes.heading: Travel
themes.text: You need a visa to enter Gabon. You'll also need a letter of invitation or sponsorship from your accommodation or sponsoring partner outlining the purpose of your visit and the duration of your stay. The letter should be in French and contain the author's contact details. You'll need to show the letter to immigration officials on arrival. Entry and exit conditions can change at short notice. Monitor local media for updates and check with your travel provider. Contact the nearest embassy or consulate of Gabon for the latest details. Restrictions and government requirements for internal travel within Gabon may change without notice. Seek local advice before internal travel, particularly when visiting areas not frequented by tourists. Carry proof of your yellow fever vaccinations. Gabon is a largely cash-based economy. The local currency is the Central African CFA franc (XAF). Credit cards aren't widely accepted. There are ATMs in major centres. Piracy and armed robbery occur in Gabon's coastal waters and the Gulf of Guinea. Check International Maritime Bureau piracy reports before you travel. Full travel advice: Travel

themes.heading: Local contacts
themes.text: The Australian Government aims to provide high quality consular assistance. There are limits to what we can do. Read the Consular Services Charter to understand how we can assist you overseas. Australia doesn't have an embassy or consulate in Gabon. The Canadian High Commission in Cameroon provides limited consular assistance to Australians with the assistance of their Honorary Consul in Libreville. For full consular assistance, contact the Australian High Commission in Nigeria. To stay up to date with local information, follow the Australian High Commission in Nigeria's social media accounts. Full travel advice: Local contacts

themes.heading: Crime
themes.text: Petty crime is common, including: pickpocketing bag snatching vehicle break-ins Hotspots for thieves include crowded: markets transport hubs tourist areas

themes.heading: Violent crime
themes.text: There have been reports of violent crime, especially in Libreville and Port Gentil. Incidents include robberies and armed attacks. Travellers have been victims of: carjackings 'snatch–and-grab' robberies from unlocked cars violent road rage Victims have been targeted when walking alone or at night. This is more common in isolated areas or on beaches. There are reports of credit card fraud originating in Gabon. To protect yourself from violent crime: do not walk alone or visit beaches and isolated areas alone, especially at night always keep car windows and doors locked, even when moving watch your credit card at all times, including during transactions monitor local media for updates about safety or security risks If you're a victim of violent crime, including rape, go to the doctor or a hospital straight away. HIV/AIDS is common in Gabon.

themes.heading: Kidnapping
themes.text: Kidnapping can happen anywhere, anytime, including in destinations that are typically at lower risk. The Australian Government's longstanding policy is that it doesn't make payments or concessions to kidnappers. More information: Kidnapping

themes.heading: Civil unrest and political tension
themes.text: Ensure you carry identification and evidence of the purpose of your travel. Local authorities may inspect your documentation.

themes.heading: Demonstrations and protests
themes.text: Public protests and events that draw large groups of people may occur with little warning and can turn violent. Demonstrations are more common in Libreville and Port Gentil. To protect yourself during periods of unrest: avoid demonstrations, protests, large gatherings and government buildings monitor local media avoid affected areas follow the advice of local authorities More information: Demonstrations

themes.heading: Terrorism
themes.text: Terrorism is a threat worldwide. Terrorist attacks could occur anywhere and at any time. Be alert to possible threats, especially in public places. Possible targets include: places of worship or religious holidays hotels, clubs, restaurants and cafes markets and shopping malls outdoor recreation events, including festivals, concerts, and sporting venues public transport, airports and railway hubs To stay safe: report suspicious activities or items to the police check the media for new threats take official warnings seriously If there’s a terrorist incident, leave the area if it’s safe to do so and follow the instructions of local authorities. More information: Terrorism

themes.heading: Extreme weather and natural disasters
themes.text: Gabon experiences natural disasters and severe weather. Flooding occurs in the rainy seasons from: October to mid-December mid-February to May You may need a 4WD on some roads at this time. If a natural disaster occurs: secure your passport in a safe, waterproof location monitor local media and other sources follow the advice of local authorities keep in contact with friends and family More information: Global Disaster Alert and Coordination System

themes.heading: Travel insurance
themes.text: Get comprehensive travel insurance before you leave and closely read the Product Disclosure Statement (PDS) for what is and isn’t covered. It must cover all medical costs overseas, including evacuation. The Australian Government will not pay for these costs. If you can’t afford travel insurance, you shouldn’t travel. This is true for everyone, even if you are healthy and fit. If you don’t have insurance, you might have to pay thousands of dollars up front for medical care. Before you travel, check your policy, make sure: you know what activities and medical care your insurance covers, including evacuations your insurance covers you for your whole trip, including any stopovers you know any exclusions – for example, you might need to pay extra for activities like motorcycling, snow sports, scuba diving or bungee jumping. More information: Travel insurance

themes.heading: Physical and mental health
themes.text: Travelling overseas can be stressful and uncertain. You may experience unfamiliar environments, travel disruptions, changes in normal routines and limited access to health care. Not taking your prescribed medications is a common cause of mental ill-health for Australians overseas. If you are concerned about your welfare, or someone else's, contact your nearest Australian embassy, high commission, or consulate. You can also call the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on +61 2 6261 3305. Mental health resources: Information and advice on mental illness (HealthDirect). Living with mental illness (BeyondBlue) Medicare Mental Health (the Department of Health and Aged Care) More information: General health advice Mental health support overseas

themes.heading: Insect-borne diseases
themes.text: Yellow fever is widespread in Gabon. Yellow fever is a potentially fatal virus spread by mosquitoes. It's prevented by vaccination. Get vaccinated before you travel. Malaria is common throughout the year. Other insect-borne diseases include: chikungunya dengue zika virus filariasis African sleeping sickness If you're pregnant, the Australian Department of Health and Aged Care recommends that you: discuss your travel plans with your doctor consider deferring non-essential travel to zika-affected areas To protect yourself from disease: make sure your accommodation is insect-proof use treated mosquito nets and insect repellent wear long, loose, light-coloured clothing Consider taking medication to prevent malaria. Seek medical advice if you have a fever, muscle pain, rash or severe headache.

themes.heading: HIV/AIDS
themes.text: HIV/AIDS is common in Gabon. Take precautions if taking part in activities that put you at risk of infection.

themes.heading: Other health risks
themes.text: Waterborne, foodborne, parasitic, and other infectious diseases are common. These include: cholera typhoid hepatitis tuberculosis rabies Serious outbreaks sometimes occur. To protect yourself from illness: drink boiled water or bottled water with sealed lids avoid ice cubes avoid raw and undercooked food, such as salads don't swim in fresh water avoid contact with dogs and other mammals If you're bitten or scratched by an animal, get medical help straight away. Get medical advice if you have a fever or diarrhoea.

themes.heading: Medical care
themes.text: There’s no agreement between Australia and Gabon for free healthcare. Tourists must pay for all medical care, even if they need to stay in hospital or be evacuated. This can be very expensive. Make sure your travel insurance covers you. The Australian Government won't pay for these costs. Medical facilities are adequate in major cities but basic or unavailable in rural areas. You'll need to pay up-front before doctors and hospitals will treat you.

themes.heading: Drugs
themes.text: Penalties for drug offences are severe and can include long prison sentences in local jails. More information: Carrying or using drugs

themes.heading: LGBTQIA+ information
themes.text: Same-sex relationships aren't legally recognised. Local communities can be intolerant towards same-sex relationships, so avoid public displays of affection. There have been attempts to charge people in same-sex marriages under public order and obscenity laws. More information: Advice for LGBTQIA+ travellers

themes.heading: Visas and border measures
themes.text: Each country or territory decides its own entry and exit rules, including health border screening measures. To find out what you need to enter a destination, ask their embassy, consulate or immigration office.

themes.heading: Visa conditions
themes.text: You should get a visa to enter Gabon. Those entering Gabon under visa-free arrangements have experienced immigration issues during their stay. You also need a letter of invitation or sponsorship from your accommodation or sponsoring partner outlining the purpose of your visit and the duration of your stay. The letter should be in French and contain the author's contact details. You'll need to show the letter to immigration officials on arrival. Gabon doesn't have an embassy or consulate in Australia. Gabon's nearest embassy is in Tokyo. Entry and exit conditions can change at short notice. Contact the nearest embassy or consulate for the latest details about visas, currency, customs and other travel requirements.

themes.heading: Yellow fever vaccination
themes.text: You'll need a valid yellow fever vaccination certificate to enter Gabon. Some airlines may want to see one when you leave. Find out about returning to Australia after exposure to yellow fever. More information: Countries with a risk of yellow fever

themes.heading: Lost or stolen passport
themes.text: Your passport is valuable and can be used by others to commit crimes. Don’t let anyone trick you into giving it to them. Always keep it in a safe place. If your passport is lost or stolen, tell the Australian Government: In Australia: contact the Australian Passport Information Service. Overseas: contact the nearest Australian embassy or consulate.

themes.heading: Passport with 'X' gender identifier
themes.text: Australian passports comply with international standards for sex and gender. If your passport has an 'X' in the sex field, we can’t guarantee your entry or transit to other countries. Ask the embassy or consulate of your destinations if they’ll accept your passport with an 'X' gender marker. Check all transit destinations too. More information: LGBTQIA+ travellers

themes.heading: Money
themes.text: The local currency is the Central African CFA Franc (XAF). Declare all amounts of local or foreign currency more than XAF200,000 on arrival and departure. This covers all forms of currency, not only cash. You can only cash traveller's cheques at larger banks in major cities. The cheques must be in either euros or US dollars. Gabon is largely a cash-based economy. Credit cards aren't widely accepted, except at major hotels and restaurants. Ask your bank if your cards will work in Gabon. You'll find ATMs in major centres. Take care when using ATMs because thieves may target you. See the Safety section for more information.

themes.heading: Driving permit
themes.text: To drive in Gabon, you need both: a valid Australian driver's licence (digital driver's licences may not be accepted) an International Driving Permit (IDP) You must obtain your IDP before leaving Australia.

themes.heading: Road travel
themes.text: Hazards include: poorly maintained roads poorly maintained and overloaded vehicles local driving practices poor lighting and road signs, especially outside urban areas wildlife such as elephants may wander onto roads, particularly at night Travel outside of major urban centres usually requires a 4WD vehicle. Towing and repair services aren't common outside Libreville. There are police roadblocks throughout the country. You may be asked to show your identity and motor vehicle registration papers. Check you have adequate travel insurance coverage before driving. If you hire a vehicle, confirm with your rental company what you're required to carry in your vehicle, such as: licensing documents proof of insurance safety equipment such as a fire extinguisher To reduce your risks if you plan to drive: get to know local traffic laws and practices be alert to pedestrians, animals and other hazards on the road avoid travelling at night get local advice on road conditions, especially during the rainy season If you're involved in a traffic accident, go to the nearest police station to avoid possible confrontations. More information: Driving or riding

themes.heading: Motorcycles
themes.text: Your travel insurance policy may not cover you when riding a motorbike, quad bike or similar vehicle. Always wear a helmet.

themes.heading: Taxis
themes.text: Taxis are generally safe but often pick up multiple passengers and take indirect routes. Local transport apps are available, however none of the major international rideshare services operate in Gabon. To reduce risks when using taxis: negotiate the fare with the driver before entering the taxi avoid using taxis alone or at night ask the driver not to pick up other passengers only use authorised taxis use hotel taxi and limousine services if they're available

themes.heading: Public transport
themes.text: Buses and trains in Gabon are reasonably safe. Services are infrequent. More information: Transport and getting around safely

themes.heading: Air travel
themes.text: DFAT doesn’t give advice about how safe different airlines or flight paths are. Safety risks and weather events may cause flight delays and cancellations. Contact your travel agent or airline to confirm your plans. Airlines and airports can restrict what you can take in your luggage. Regulations may be different to those in Australia. Check with your airline before you travel. Check Gabon's air safety profile with the Aviation Safety Network. More information: Air travel

themes.heading: Medical emergencies
themes.text: Call 177 or go to the hospital. Depending on your mobile network, call: 1300, 0174 or 0880 for SOS Médecins. Operators may only speak French.

themes.heading: Consular contacts
themes.text: The Australian Government aims to provide high quality consular assistance. There are limits to what we can do. Read the Consular Services Charter to understand how we can assist you overseas. Australia doesn't have an embassy in Gabon. The Canadian High Commission in Cameroon provides limited consular assistance to Australians in Gabon. It can issue Australian provisional travel documents. Limited consular services are also available from the Australian High Commission in Nigeria.

themes.heading: 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre
themes.text: In a consular emergency, if you can't contact an embassy, call the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on: +61 2 6261 3305 from overseas 1300 555 135 in Australia

themes.heading: 기본정보 (basisinformatie)
themes.text: ㅇ 국명 : 가봉 공화국(République Gabonaise) ㅇ 위치 : 아프리카 중서부 적도 ㅇ 인구 : 210만명(‘19년 UN추산) ㅇ 기후 : 열대성 기후, 연중 고온다습 ㅇ 면적 : 267,667㎢ (한반도의 1.2배) ㅇ 인종 : Fang(다수족), Echira, Punu, Kota, Teke 등 40여 종족 ㅇ 언어 : 불어(공용어), 토착어 (Fang, Echira 등) ㅇ 수도 : 리브르빌(Libreville, 인구 67만) ㅇ 주요 도시 : 포르장티(Port-Gentil, 제2도시, 수출입항), 프랑스빌(Franceville) ㅇ 종교 : 기독교(천주교 포함) 85%, 이슬람교(9.8%), 토착신앙 등

themes.heading: 사건ㆍ사고 현황
themes.text: [천재지변, 전쟁, 내란, 테러 등에 대한 상황 및 정세] ㅇ 가봉(Gabonese Republic)은 아프리카 중부 서해안, 대서양에 접하고 적도 바로 밑에 위치, 동쪽과 남쪽은 콩고 공화국, 북쪽은 카메룬 및 적도기니와 인접하고 있습니다. ㅇ 가봉은 1960년대 중반 이후 풍부한 천연자원(석유, 망간, 목재, 우라늄)에 힘입어 아프리카 국가 중 개인 소득 수준이 적도기니에 이어 두 번째로 높습니다. ㅇ 오마르 봉고 전 대통령의 장기 집권(41년) 이래 인근 지역 중 정세가 가장 안정된 편이나, 알리 봉고 현 대통령이 국외 요양 중 2019.1.7.(월) 수도 리브르빌에서 군사 쿠데타가 발생하여 수 시간 만에 진압되는 등, 현 정부에 대한 야당 및 국민들의 불만으로 치안 혼란 상황이 발생할 가능성은 있음 [살인, 강도, 납치 등 범죄 피해 가능성 등 치안상태] ㅇ 인접국가와 특별한 분쟁은 없으며 테러 등 치안상태 역시 비교적 양호한 편이나 시내를 벗어난 현지인 밀집 거주 지역 출입은 자제가 요망됩니다. ㅇ 가난한 주변국으로부터 외국인 노동인력 유입에 대처하고 자국인 고용증가를 위하여 외국인 체류증 발급 제한과, 국경 검문검색 강화, 시내 차량 불심검문 등으로 외국인 유입을 제한하고 있습니다. 여행자는 여권, 운전자는 면허증을 반드시 휴대하도록 하고 검문시 제시하여야 합니다. ㅇ 외국인 노동자 장기 체류 요건이 미비한 경우 자국으로 추방하고 있으며, 이들 노동자 대부분 택시 운전사, 건설노동자, 가정부, 경비원 등으로 취업하여 근무하고 있습니다. ㅇ 외국인 불법체류자는 자국인에 비해 낮은 임금과 불리한 근로조건 등으로 장기적인 사회적 불안요인으로 작용하고 있으며, 이들을 통해 노상강도, 택시 강절도, 가택침입 절도, 권총강도 등의 범죄 사례가 발생하고 있습니다. ㅇ 기니만 해역에 해적에 의한 공격 및 피랍사건이 발생하고 있어 각별한 주의를 필요로 합니다. ㅇ 현재 코로나19 사태의 장기화와 그로 인한 불경기 등을 배경으로 아시아계에 대한 언어적·신체적 폭력 사건이 발생할 가능성이 있으므로, 신변안전에 유의하여야 합니다.

themes.heading: 사건ㆍ사고의 유형
themes.text: ㅇ음주단속을 하지 않지만, 주재국 경찰의 불심검문이 자주 있으며 각종 차량 내 구비 서류 및 물품을 확인하는 경우가 많습니다. - 대처 : 늦은 귀가시 가족에게 전화 등으로 미리 위치를 알리고, 현금 및 귀중품은 차량에 보관하지 않도록 합니다. ㅇ 자동차 사고가 발생한 경우 사고 장소에서 집단 폭행을 당할 가능성이 있으므로 사태 수습을 하지 말고 신속히 현장을 벗어나 신 후 즉시 경찰서에 신고하시기 바랍니다.

themes.heading: 자연재해
themes.text: ㅇ 대우기철(2월~6월) 천둥, 폭우에 의한 도로 유실로 차량 이동이 어려운 경우가 많이 발생하고 있습니다.

themes.heading: 유의해야 할 지역
themes.text: ㅇ 수도인 리브르빌 시내는 상대적으로 치안이 양호한 편이나 현지인 밀집 지역(예 :오깔라 Okala)을 출입할 경우에는 각별한 주의가 필요합니다. 특히 아시아인의 경우 조롱 및 시비, 금품갈취, 폭행의 대상이 될 수 있으니 늦은 시간 출입은 자제하시기 바랍니다.

themes.heading: 출입국 (in- en uitreis)
themes.text: 무비자 입국 가능 여부(1회 입국시 최대 연속 체류 가능 일자) ㅇ 외교관 여권 소지자 : O, (외교목적: 임무기간, 그 외의 경우 90일) (협정) ㅇ 관용여권 소지자 : O, (관용목적: 임무기간, 그 외의 경우 90일) (협정) ㅇ 일반여권 소지자(복수와 단수를 구분할 경우 별도 표기 필요) : X ㅇ 도착사증 발급 여부 : 도착사증 발급 • E-VISA 시스템을 통해 비자 신청한 후, 현지 도착하여 수수료 지불하고 비자 발급 가능 (자세한 내용은 dgdi.ga 참조) - 기간 : 단수 비자 : 최대 3개월, 복수 비자 : 최대 6개월 - 수수료 : 단수 : 45 000 FCFA(€70) + €15, 복수 : 120,000FCFA(€200) + €15 • 구비서류 :유효한 여권, 증명사진 1매, 공식적인 행사관련 규정에 맞게 작성된 초대장, 서류이민국에서 사전에 발급한 입국허가서를 5분내에 제출 입국시 유의사항 등 ㅇ 1,000,000 CFA (약 1,800 미불) 이상은 의무 신고 대상 출국시 유의사항 등 - 참고자료 ㅇ 해당 국가의 주한공관홈페이지 : www.amba-coree.ga ㅇ 해당 국가의 한국 대사관 홈페이지 : overseas.mofa.go.kr/ga-ko/index.do ㅇ 기타 참고자료 : - 가봉외교부 : www.diplomatie.gouv.ga - 가봉서류이민국 : dgdi.ga - 항공국경경찰국 : anacgabon.org

themes.heading: 일반 문화
themes.text: ㅇ 국민성은 개방적이고, 처음 보는 사람이라도 인사(Bonjour)를 합니다. ㅇ 사진촬영은 군사시설 등 금지된 지역 외는 가능하나 일반 시민의 사진을 찍는 것은 사전에 양해를 구하는 것이 좋습니다.

themes.heading: 종교 관련
themes.text: ㅇ 이슬람 신자가 많은 관계로 규칙적으로 기도 시간을 갖는 무슬림이 많습니다. 무슬림은 돼지고기를 먹지 않으므로 해당 종교인을 만나 식사를 대접할 경우 돼지고기를 피하는 것이 좋습니다. 라마단이라는 단식 기간 동안에는 식사를 자제하므로 억지로 권하지 않는 것이 좋습니다.

themes.heading: 팁 문화
themes.text: ㅇ 가봉은 호텔, 식당 서비스 이용요금에 팁이 포함되어 있으며, 호텔이나 공항에서 짐을 이동해 주는 경우에는 보통 $1.5 정도의 현지화(750~1000CFA)를 지불하면 됩니다. ㅇ 일반적인 식당의 경우 팁이 의무는 아니지만, 고급 식당의 경우 1500~2500CFA(미화 $2.5~4.3) 정도 팁을 주기도 합니다.

themes.heading: 주요 교통 법규 및 문화
themes.text: ㅇ 주요 교통 법규는 한국 및 유럽과 유사합니다. 다만 우기(9월말~5월초)에는 빈번히 발생하는 폭우로 도로 침수, 시야 방해가 생길 수 있기 때문에 운전에 각별히 유의해야 합니다. 또한 대부분의 도로의 노면 상태가 좋지 않아 포트 홀 등이 많이 발생해 있고 야간에는 도로 조명 부족으로 충분한 시야확보가 불가능하기 때문에 과속을 삼가고 인명 사고에 유의해야 합니다. ㅇ 또한 수도 리브르빌에서도 대부분의 도로에 교통 신호등이 없지만 시내 중심지 등에는 신호등이 설치되어 있는 구간이 있기 때문에 과속하지 않는 것이 안전합니다. ㅇ 육교나 횡단보도가 드물어 보행자들의 무단 횡단이 많으므로 안전 운전에 유의해야 합니다.

themes.heading: 관련 사건사고 사례
themes.text: ㅇ 폭우로 도로가 침수된 상태에서 시야 방해로 앞서가던 차량과 추돌사고가 많이 발생하고 있습니다. ㅇ 도로 조명 부족으로 충분한 시야확보가 어렵기 때문에 무단 횡단 보행자 사망사고가 많이 발생하고 있습니다.

themes.heading: 관련 사건사고 발생시 대처방법
themes.text: ㅇ 교통사고 발생시 경찰 및 소방서에 신고하고 재외공관 긴급연락처로 연락하십시오 □ 대중교통 [버스(SOGATRA)] ㅇ 리브르빌 내 요금은 목적지에 따라 100-500CFA(약 0.2-1$)이며, 앞문 승차시 운전기사에게 현금을 지불합니다. [택시버스(TAXIBUS)] ㅇ 요금 - 1구간 300CFA(약 $0.5), 우리나라 승합차와 같은 차량으로 택시와 같이 승하차장 없이 수신호로 승차가 가능합니다. [택시(TAXI)] ㅇ 가봉 내 가장 기본적인 대중교통으로 팁 의무는 없으며 책정된 기본요금 또한 없습니다. ㅇ 시내 구간 개인당 요금 현지화 500CFA(약 $0.9), 승차시 운전기사와 목적지 및 금액을 합의한 후 승차합니다. 1) 공항에서 시내(en ville)까지 : 현지화 1,000~2,000CFA(약 $1.7~3.4), 공항 오웬도(Owendo) : 4,000CFA(약$6.9) 2) 시내에서 대사관이 있는 사블리에(Sablière) 지역까지 : 현지화 2,000CFA, 공항-대사관: 1,000CFA 3) 대사관에서 시내 주요 대형 마트 음볼로(Mbolo)까지 : 현지화 1,500~2,000CFA [대중교통 이용 시 주의 사항] ㅇ 외국인, 노약자 및 여성은 대중교통 이용 시 차량 강도를 주의하셔야 합니다. ㅇ 늦은 시간 택시 이용은 가급적 자제해 주시기 바라며, 불가피하게 이용할 경우 반드시 동행 2~3인과 함께 탑승하시기 바랍니다. ㅇ 최근 2~3년간 외국인 노동자의 증가로 인하여 택시 강도 및 납치 사건이 빈번하게 발생하고 있으니 주의하시기 바랍니다. ㅇ 아시아인을 대상으로 과한 승차 금액을 요구하기도 하오니 운전사가 본인이 제시한 금액보다 터무니 없이 높은 가격을 부를 경우 지불하지 마시고 해당 택시를 보내신 후 다른 택시에 승차하시기 바랍니다. [도로교통] ㅇ 운전 시 운전면허증을 항시 소지하고, 불시 검문을 대비하여 차량 내 필수 구비 서류 및 물품 은 차량에 비치하시기 바랍니다. - 필수 구비 서류 및 물품 : 운전면허증, 차량 보험증, 자동차등록증, 소화기,차량용 안전삼각대,구급함,타이어 지지대,자동차 정기검사 확인증,손전등 등 9가지 ㅇ 프랑스식으로 오른쪽 진입차량이 우선권을 가집니다. ㅇ 도로별 속도제한은 고속도로 110~120km/h, 시내 도로 40~60km/h입니다. ㅇ 앞 좌석에 앉은 사람은 반드시 안전벨트를 착용해야 하며 미착용시 검문 및 벌금의 대상이 됩니다. ㅇ 운전 시 휴대전화를 사용하는 운전자는 벌금의 대상이 되오니 운전 중 휴대전화 사용은 자제하여 주시기 바랍니다. ㅇ 리브르빌 시내 전 지역 도로는 대체로 잘 포장되어 있음 □ 항공 ㅇ ADL S.A (+241) 1173-6244) https://www.libreville-aeroport.com(가봉 도시별 시간, 편명 조회 가능) [국내선] ㅇ AFRIJET (+241) 7756-0303 https://www.flyafrijet.online [국외선] ㅇ 에어프랑스 (+241) 1179-6464 https://www.airfrance.ga ㅇ 에티오피아 항공 (+241) 1174-1315 https://www.ethiopianairlines.com ㅇ 터키 항공 (+241) 1176-3535 https://www.turkishairlines.com/ ㅇ 코트디부아르 항공 (+241) 1177-0560 https://www.aircotedivoire.com ㅇ 르완다항공 (+241) 1176-4882 https://www.rwandair.com ㅇ ASKY 항공 (+241) 6222-8500 https://www.flyasky.com ㅇ 로열에어 모로코 (+241) 1173-1025 https://www.royalairmaroc.com ㅇ 세네갈항공 https://flyairsenegal.com ㅇ 에어 부르키나 http://www.air-burkina.com/ ㅇ 모리타니 항공 https://www.mauritaniaairline.com/

themes.heading: 기타 유의사항
themes.text: ㅇ 수도 리브르빌 외곽에서부터 지방으로 연결되는 도로의 포장 상태가 좋지 않기 때문에 차량의 사전 점검이 꼭 필요하며, 도로의 포장이 끊긴 구간이 많이 있다는 점을 참고할 필요가 있습니다.

themes.heading: 날씨
themes.text: ㅇ 열대우림 기후로 건기와 우기가 있으며 여행은 기온이 서늘하고 비가 덜 오는 건기철이 바람직합니다. - 대우기 : 3~6월 - 대건기 : 6~9월 - 소우기 : 10~12월 - 소건기 : 1~3월 ㅇ 우기철은 고온다습하며 천둥번개를 동반한 폭우가 내립니다. 건기철은 비가 거의 오지 않으나 말라리아모기와 각종 병해충의 위험이 있사오니 건기철 여행시 말라리아 감염에 각별히 유의하시기 바랍니다. ㅇ 연중 평균 기온은 섭씨 25~28도이며, 최저기온은 7월 18도, 최고기온은 4월 35도입니다. ㅇ 날씨정보 사이트 : http://www.wunderground.com/weather-forecast/GO/Libreville.html (한국어 지원)

themes.heading: 기타
themes.text: [한국과의 시차] ㅇ한국이 8시간 빠르며 서머타임은 없습니다.(예 : 가봉 1월 1일 오전 8시=한국 1월 1일 오후 16시) [전력 사용 현황] ㅇ 220V ㅇ 한국 전자제품의 대부분이 사용 가능하며, 만약의 경우를 대비하여 여행 시 소형 멀티 어댑터 소지를 권장합니다.
